circuits.BaseCircuitType.name: BaseCircuitType
circuits.BaseCircuitType.docstring: Abstract base model to represent a type of physical or virtual circuit. Circuits can be organized by their functional role. For example, a user might wish to define CircuitTypes named "Long Haul," "Me

circuits.CircuitType.name: CircuitType
circuits.CircuitType.docstring: Circuits can be organized by their functional role. For example, a user might wish to define CircuitTypes named "Long Haul," "Metro," or "Out-of-Band".

circuits.Circuit.name: Circuit
circuits.Circuit.docstring: A communications circuit connects two points. Each Circuit belongs to a Provider; Providers may have multiple circuits. Each circuit is also assigned a CircuitType and a Site, and may optionally be as

circuits.CircuitGroupAssignment.name: CircuitGroupAssignment
circuits.CircuitGroupAssignment.docstring: Assignment of a physical or virtual circuit to a CircuitGroup with an optional priority.

circuits.Provider.name: Provider
circuits.Provider.docstring: Each Circuit belongs to a Provider. This is usually a telecommunications company or similar organization. This model stores information pertinent to the user's relationship with the Provider.

circuits.ProviderAccount.name: ProviderAccount
circuits.ProviderAccount.docstring: This is a discrete account within a provider. Each Circuit belongs to a Provider Account.

circuits.ProviderNetwork.name: ProviderNetwork
circuits.ProviderNetwork.docstring: This represents a provider network which exists outside of NetBox, the details of which are unknown or unimportant to the user.

circuits.VirtualCircuitType.name: VirtualCircuitType
circuits.VirtualCircuitType.docstring: Like physical circuits, virtual circuits can be organized by their functional role. For example, a user might wish to categorize virtual circuits by their technological nature or by product name.

circuits.VirtualCircuit.name: VirtualCircuit
circuits.VirtualCircuit.docstring: A virtual connection between two or more endpoints, delivered across one or more physical circuits.

core.ObjectChange.name: ObjectChange
core.ObjectChange.docstring: Record a change to an object and the user account associated with that change. A change record may optionally indicate an object related to the one being changed. For example, a change to an interface

core.ConfigRevision.name: ConfigRevision
core.ConfigRevision.docstring: An atomic revision of NetBox's configuration.

core.DataSource.name: DataSource
core.DataSource.docstring: A remote source, such as a git repository, from which DataFiles are synchronized.

core.DataFile.name: DataFile
core.DataFile.docstring: The database representation of a remote file fetched from a remote DataSource. DataFile instances should be created, updated, or deleted only by calling DataSource.sync().

core.AutoSyncRecord.name: AutoSyncRecord
core.AutoSyncRecord.docstring: Maps a DataFile to a synced object for efficient automatic updating.

core.ManagedFile.name: ManagedFile
core.ManagedFile.docstring: Database representation for a file on disk. This class is typically wrapped by a proxy class (e.g. ScriptModule) to provide additional functionality.

core.Job.name: Job
core.Job.docstring: Tracks the lifecycle of a job which represents a background task (e.g. the execution of a custom script).

core.ObjectType.name: ObjectType
core.ObjectType.docstring: Wrap Django's native ContentType model to use our custom manager.

dcim.PortMappingBase.name: PortMappingBase
dcim.PortMappingBase.docstring: Base class for PortMapping and PortTemplateMapping

dcim.Cable.name: Cable
dcim.Cable.docstring: A physical connection between two endpoints.

dcim.CableTermination.name: CableTermination
dcim.CableTermination.docstring: A mapping between side A or B of a Cable and a terminating object (e.g. an Interface or CircuitTermination).

dcim.CablePath.name: CablePath
dcim.CablePath.docstring: A CablePath instance represents the physical path from a set of origin nodes to a set of destination nodes, including all intermediate elements. `path` contains the ordered set of nodes, arranged in l

dcim.ModularComponentTemplateModel.name: ModularComponentTemplateModel
dcim.ModularComponentTemplateModel.docstring: A ComponentTemplateModel which supports optional assignment to a ModuleType.

dcim.ConsolePortTemplate.name: ConsolePortTemplate
dcim.ConsolePortTemplate.docstring: A template for a ConsolePort to be created for a new Device.

dcim.ConsoleServerPortTemplate.name: ConsoleServerPortTemplate
dcim.ConsoleServerPortTemplate.docstring: A template for a ConsoleServerPort to be created for a new Device.

dcim.PowerPortTemplate.name: PowerPortTemplate
dcim.PowerPortTemplate.docstring: A template for a PowerPort to be created for a new Device.

dcim.PowerOutletTemplate.name: PowerOutletTemplate
dcim.PowerOutletTemplate.docstring: A template for a PowerOutlet to be created for a new Device.

dcim.InterfaceTemplate.name: InterfaceTemplate
dcim.InterfaceTemplate.docstring: A template for a physical data interface on a new Device.

dcim.PortTemplateMapping.name: PortTemplateMapping
dcim.PortTemplateMapping.docstring: Maps a FrontPortTemplate & position to a RearPortTemplate & position.

dcim.FrontPortTemplate.name: FrontPortTemplate
dcim.FrontPortTemplate.docstring: Template for a pass-through port on the front of a new Device.

dcim.RearPortTemplate.name: RearPortTemplate
dcim.RearPortTemplate.docstring: Template for a pass-through port on the rear of a new Device.

dcim.ModuleBayTemplate.name: ModuleBayTemplate
dcim.ModuleBayTemplate.docstring: A template for a ModuleBay to be created for a new parent Device.

dcim.DeviceBayTemplate.name: DeviceBayTemplate
dcim.DeviceBayTemplate.docstring: A template for a DeviceBay to be created for a new parent Device.

dcim.InventoryItemTemplate.name: InventoryItemTemplate
dcim.InventoryItemTemplate.docstring: A template for an InventoryItem to be created for a new parent Device.

dcim.ComponentModel.name: ComponentModel
dcim.ComponentModel.docstring: An abstract model inherited by any model which has a parent Device.

dcim.CabledObjectModel.name: CabledObjectModel
dcim.CabledObjectModel.docstring: An abstract model inherited by all models to which a Cable can terminate. Provides the `cable` and `cable_end` fields for caching cable associations, as well as `mark_connected` to designate "fake" co

dcim.PathEndpoint.name: PathEndpoint
dcim.PathEndpoint.docstring: An abstract model inherited by any CabledObjectModel subclass which represents the end of a CablePath; specifically, these include ConsolePort, ConsoleServerPort, PowerPort, PowerOutlet, Interface, an

dcim.ConsolePort.name: ConsolePort
dcim.ConsolePort.docstring: A physical console port within a Device. ConsolePorts connect to ConsoleServerPorts.

dcim.ConsoleServerPort.name: ConsoleServerPort
dcim.ConsoleServerPort.docstring: A physical port within a Device (typically a designated console server) which provides access to ConsolePorts.

dcim.PowerPort.name: PowerPort
dcim.PowerPort.docstring: A physical power supply (intake) port within a Device. PowerPorts connect to PowerOutlets.

dcim.PowerOutlet.name: PowerOutlet
dcim.PowerOutlet.docstring: A physical power outlet (output) within a Device which provides power to a PowerPort.

dcim.BaseInterface.name: BaseInterface
dcim.BaseInterface.docstring: Abstract base class for fields shared by dcim.Interface and virtualization.VMInterface.

dcim.Interface.name: Interface
dcim.Interface.docstring: A network interface within a Device. A physical Interface can connect to exactly one other Interface.

dcim.PortMapping.name: PortMapping
dcim.PortMapping.docstring: Maps a FrontPort & position to a RearPort & position.

dcim.FrontPort.name: FrontPort
dcim.FrontPort.docstring: A pass-through port on the front of a Device.

dcim.RearPort.name: RearPort
dcim.RearPort.docstring: A pass-through port on the rear of a Device.

dcim.ModuleBay.name: ModuleBay
dcim.ModuleBay.docstring: An empty space within a Device which can house a child device

dcim.DeviceBay.name: DeviceBay
dcim.DeviceBay.docstring: An empty space within a Device which can house a child device

dcim.InventoryItemRole.name: InventoryItemRole
dcim.InventoryItemRole.docstring: Inventory items may optionally be assigned a functional role.

dcim.InventoryItem.name: InventoryItem
dcim.InventoryItem.docstring: An InventoryItem represents a serialized piece of hardware within a Device, such as a line card or power supply. InventoryItems are used only for inventory purposes.

dcim.Manufacturer.name: Manufacturer
dcim.Manufacturer.docstring: A Manufacturer represents a company which produces hardware devices; for example, Juniper or Dell.

dcim.DeviceType.name: DeviceType
dcim.DeviceType.docstring: A DeviceType represents a particular make (Manufacturer) and model of device. It specifies rack height and depth, as well as high-level functional role(s). Each DeviceType can have an arbitrary number

dcim.DeviceRole.name: DeviceRole
dcim.DeviceRole.docstring: Devices are organized by functional role; for example, "Core Switch" or "File Server". Each DeviceRole is assigned a color to be used when displaying rack elevations. The vm_role field determines whet

dcim.Platform.name: Platform
dcim.Platform.docstring: Platform refers to the software or firmware running on a Device. For example, "Cisco IOS-XR" or "Juniper Junos". A Platform may optionally be associated with a particular Manufacturer.

dcim.Device.name: Device
dcim.Device.docstring: A Device represents a piece of physical hardware mounted within a Rack. Each Device is assigned a DeviceType, DeviceRole, and (optionally) a Platform. Device names are not required, however if one is

dcim.VirtualChassis.name: VirtualChassis
dcim.VirtualChassis.docstring: A collection of Devices which operate with a shared control plane (e.g. a switch stack).

dcim.CachedScopeMixin.name: CachedScopeMixin
dcim.CachedScopeMixin.docstring: Mixin for adding a GenericForeignKey scope to a model that can point to a Region, SiteGroup, Site, or Location. Includes cached fields for each to allow efficient filtering. Appropriate validation mus

dcim.ModuleTypeProfile.name: ModuleTypeProfile
dcim.ModuleTypeProfile.docstring: A profile which defines the attributes which can be set on one or more ModuleTypes.

dcim.ModuleType.name: ModuleType
dcim.ModuleType.docstring: A ModuleType represents a hardware element that can be installed within a device and which houses additional components; for example, a line card within a chassis-based switch such as the Cisco Cataly

dcim.Module.name: Module
dcim.Module.docstring: A Module represents a field-installable component within a Device which may itself hold multiple device components (for example, a line card within a chassis switch). Modules are instantiated from Mod

dcim.PowerPanel.name: PowerPanel
dcim.PowerPanel.docstring: A distribution point for electrical power; e.g. a data center RPP.

dcim.PowerFeed.name: PowerFeed
dcim.PowerFeed.docstring: An electrical circuit delivered from a PowerPanel.

dcim.RackBase.name: RackBase
dcim.RackBase.docstring: Base class for RackType & Rack. Holds

dcim.RackType.name: RackType
dcim.RackType.docstring: Devices are housed within Racks. Each rack has a defined height measured in rack units, and a front and rear face. Each Rack is assigned to a Site and (optionally) a Location.

dcim.RackRole.name: RackRole
dcim.RackRole.docstring: Racks can be organized by functional role, similar to Devices.

dcim.Rack.name: Rack
dcim.Rack.docstring: Devices are housed within Racks. Each rack has a defined height measured in rack units, and a front and rear face. Each Rack is assigned to a Site and (optionally) a Location.

dcim.RackReservation.name: RackReservation
dcim.RackReservation.docstring: One or more reserved units within a Rack.

dcim.Region.name: Region
dcim.Region.docstring: A region represents a geographic collection of sites. For example, you might create regions representing countries, states, and/or cities. Regions are recursively nested into a hierarchy: all sites be

dcim.SiteGroup.name: SiteGroup
dcim.SiteGroup.docstring: A site group is an arbitrary grouping of sites. For example, you might have corporate sites and customer sites; and within corporate sites you might distinguish between offices and data centers. Like

dcim.Site.name: Site
dcim.Site.docstring: A Site represents a geographic location within a network; typically a building or campus. The optional facility field can be used to include an external designation, such as a data center name (e.g. E

dcim.Location.name: Location
dcim.Location.docstring: A Location represents a subgroup of Racks and/or Devices within a Site. A Location may represent a building within a site, or a room within a building, for example.

extras.ConfigContextProfile.name: ConfigContextProfile
extras.ConfigContextProfile.docstring: A profile which can be used to enforce parameters on a ConfigContext.

extras.ConfigContext.name: ConfigContext
extras.ConfigContext.docstring: A ConfigContext represents a set of arbitrary data available to any Device or VirtualMachine matching its assigned qualifiers (region, site, etc.). For example, the data stored in a ConfigContext assi

extras.ConfigContextModel.name: ConfigContextModel
extras.ConfigContextModel.docstring: A model which includes local configuration context data. This local data will override any inherited data from ConfigContexts.

extras.CustomFieldChoiceSet.name: CustomFieldChoiceSet
extras.CustomFieldChoiceSet.docstring: Represents a set of choices available for choice and multi-choice custom fields.

extras.CustomStoragesLoader.name: CustomStoragesLoader
extras.CustomStoragesLoader.docstring: Custom loader for exec_module to use django-storages instead of the file system.

extras.EventRule.name: EventRule
extras.EventRule.docstring: An EventRule defines an action to be taken automatically in response to a specific set of events, such as when a specific type of object is created, modified, or deleted. The action to be taken might

extras.Webhook.name: Webhook
extras.Webhook.docstring: A Webhook defines a request that will be sent to a remote application when an object is created, updated, and/or delete in NetBox. The request will contain a representation of the object, which the re

extras.CustomLink.name: CustomLink
extras.CustomLink.docstring: A custom link to an external representation of a NetBox object. The link text and URL fields accept Jinja2 template code to be rendered with an object as context.

extras.SavedFilter.name: SavedFilter
extras.SavedFilter.docstring: A set of predefined keyword parameters that can be reused to filter for specific objects.

extras.TableConfig.name: TableConfig
extras.TableConfig.docstring: A saved configuration of columns and ordering which applies to a specific table.

extras.ImageAttachment.name: ImageAttachment
extras.ImageAttachment.docstring: An uploaded image which is associated with an object.

extras.JournalEntry.name: JournalEntry
extras.JournalEntry.docstring: A historical remark concerning an object; collectively, these form an object's journal. The journal is used to preserve historical context around an object, and complements NetBox's built-in change lo

extras.Bookmark.name: Bookmark
extras.Bookmark.docstring: An object bookmarked by a User.

extras.Notification.name: Notification
extras.Notification.docstring: A notification message for a User relating to a specific object in NetBox.

extras.NotificationGroup.name: NotificationGroup
extras.NotificationGroup.docstring: A collection of users and/or groups to be informed for certain notifications.

extras.Subscription.name: Subscription
extras.Subscription.docstring: A User's subscription to a particular object, to be notified of changes.

extras.ScriptModule.name: ScriptModule
extras.ScriptModule.docstring: Proxy model for script module files.

ipam.ASN.name: ASN
ipam.ASN.docstring: An autonomous system (AS) number is typically used to represent an independent routing domain. A site can have one or more ASNs assigned to it.

ipam.FHRPGroup.name: FHRPGroup
ipam.FHRPGroup.docstring: A grouping of next hope resolution protocol (FHRP) peers. (For instance, VRRP or HSRP.)

ipam.RIR.name: RIR
ipam.RIR.docstring: A Regional Internet Registry (RIR) is responsible for the allocation of a large portion of the global IP address space. This can be an organization like ARIN or RIPE, or a governing standard such as R

ipam.Aggregate.name: Aggregate
ipam.Aggregate.docstring: An aggregate exists at the root level of the IP address space hierarchy in NetBox. Aggregates are used to organize the hierarchy and track the overall utilization of available address space. Each Aggr

ipam.Role.name: Role
ipam.Role.docstring: A Role represents the functional role of a Prefix or VLAN; for example, "Customer," "Infrastructure," or "Management."

ipam.Prefix.name: Prefix
ipam.Prefix.docstring: A Prefix represents an IPv4 or IPv6 network, including mask length. Prefixes can optionally be scoped to certain areas and/or assigned to VRFs. A Prefix must be assigned a status and may optionally be

ipam.IPRange.name: IPRange
ipam.IPRange.docstring: A range of IP addresses, defined by start and end addresses.

ipam.IPAddress.name: IPAddress
ipam.IPAddress.docstring: An IPAddress represents an individual IPv4 or IPv6 address and its mask. The mask length should match what is configured in the real world. (Typically, only loopback interfaces are configured with /32

ipam.ServiceTemplate.name: ServiceTemplate
ipam.ServiceTemplate.docstring: A template for a Service to be applied to a device or virtual machine.

ipam.Service.name: Service
ipam.Service.docstring: A Service represents a layer-four service (e.g. HTTP or SSH) running on a Device or VirtualMachine. A Service may optionally be tied to one or more specific IPAddresses belonging to its parent.

ipam.VLANGroup.name: VLANGroup
ipam.VLANGroup.docstring: A VLAN group is an arbitrary collection of VLANs within which VLAN IDs and names must be unique. Each group must define one or more ranges of valid VLAN IDs, and may be assigned a specific scope.

ipam.VLAN.name: VLAN
ipam.VLAN.docstring: A VLAN is a distinct layer two forwarding domain identified by a 12-bit integer (1-4094). Each VLAN must be assigned to a Site, however VLAN IDs need not be unique within a Site. A VLAN may optionally

ipam.VRF.name: VRF
ipam.VRF.docstring: A virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) table represents a discrete layer three forwarding domain (e.g. a routing table). Prefixes and IPAddresses can optionally be assigned to VRFs. (Prefixes and IPAd

ipam.RouteTarget.name: RouteTarget
ipam.RouteTarget.docstring: A BGP extended community used to control the redistribution of routes among VRFs, as defined in RFC 4364.

tenancy.ContactRole.name: ContactRole
tenancy.ContactRole.docstring: Functional role for a Contact assigned to an object.

tenancy.Contact.name: Contact
tenancy.Contact.docstring: Contact information for a particular object(s) in NetBox.

tenancy.Tenant.name: Tenant
tenancy.Tenant.docstring: A Tenant represents an organization served by the NetBox owner. This is typically a customer or an internal department.

users.ObjectPermission.name: ObjectPermission
users.ObjectPermission.docstring: A mapping of view, add, change, and/or delete permission for users and/or groups to an arbitrary set of objects identified by ORM query parameters.

users.UserConfig.name: UserConfig
users.UserConfig.docstring: This model stores arbitrary user-specific preferences in a JSON data structure.

users.Token.name: Token
users.Token.docstring: An API token used for user authentication. This extends the stock model to allow each user to have multiple tokens. It also supports setting an expiration time and toggling write ability.

virtualization.Cluster.name: Cluster
virtualization.Cluster.docstring: A cluster of VirtualMachines. Each Cluster may optionally be associated with one or more Devices.

virtualization.VirtualMachine.name: VirtualMachine
virtualization.VirtualMachine.docstring: A virtual machine which runs inside a Cluster.

virtualization.ComponentModel.name: ComponentModel
virtualization.ComponentModel.docstring: An abstract model inherited by any model which has a parent VirtualMachine.

vpn.TunnelGroup.name: TunnelGroup
vpn.TunnelGroup.docstring: An administrative grouping of Tunnels. This can be used to correlate peer-to-peer tunnels which form a mesh, for example.

wireless.WirelessAuthenticationBase.name: WirelessAuthenticationBase
wireless.WirelessAuthenticationBase.docstring: Abstract model for attaching attributes related to wireless authentication.

wireless.WirelessLAN.name: WirelessLAN
wireless.WirelessLAN.docstring: A wireless network formed among an arbitrary number of access point and clients.

wireless.WirelessLink.name: WirelessLink
wireless.WirelessLink.docstring: A point-to-point connection between two wireless Interfaces.
